category_title: Students with reactive behaviors and work refusal
scenario_title: Managing weekly student diary and accountability
minimal_context: The teacher asks J to hand in his weekly student diary on the first day of the school week.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Good morning J. It is the first day of the school week, so it is time to check your weekly student diary.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Gets out his diary) I have the book.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Thank you. Do you have your diary ready for me to check the parent signature?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: Yes, but I haven’t had it signed.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: You know the expectation is to bring the diary back signed. Because it is not signed, you will lose your playtime today.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Throws a temper tantrum) It's not fair, pages were skipped and she missed it! I'm not doing any work if I lose my playtime!

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Good morning J. It is the first day of the school week, so it is time to check your weekly student diary.

STUDENT: (Gets out his diary) I have the book.

TUTOR: Thank you. Do you have your diary ready for me to check the parent signature?

STUDENT: Yes, but I haven’t had it signed.

TUTOR: You know the expectation is to bring the diary back signed. Because it is not signed, you will lose your playtime today.

STUDENT: (Throws a temper tantrum) It's not fair, pages were skipped and she missed it! I'm not doing any work if I lose my playtime!
